page.title: Dubhe Day Boat Charter Review: What It's Like Onboard in Marbella

data.meta_description: Read our honest Dubhe Marbella review: 8m day boat, 5 pax, departs Puerto Banús from €230/2h. See what it's like onboard, pricing & how to book.
data.summary: An honest review of the Dubhe, an 8-metre day boat in Marbella that departs Puerto Banús from €230 for 2 hours. It carries up to 5 passengers and is ideal for small groups wanting a simple, affordable day on the water.
key_facts: Drinks & snacks included; fuel not included (€30–€60 by route)
Licence needed to take the helm — skippered departures on request
Ideal for couples or small families
Great for exploring Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés

faq.q: Do I need a licence to drive the Dubhe in Marbella?
faq.a: The Dubhe is 8 metres, above Spain's 5 m licence-free limit, so a valid boating licence (PNB/PER, ICC or RYA Day Skipper) is needed to take the helm. No licence? Ask us on WhatsApp about a skippered departure. A full safety briefing is given before departure either way.

faq.q: How many people can the Dubhe carry?
faq.a: The Dubhe has a maximum capacity of 5 passengers. This makes it ideal for couples, small families, or a group of friends looking for an intimate day out.

faq.q: What is included in the Dubhe charter price?
faq.a: Every Dubhe charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Fuel is not included (typically €30–€60 depending on route). Pricing is published: €230 for 2 hours, €280 for 3 hours, €350 for 4 hours.

faq.q: Where does the Dubhe depart from?
faq.a: The Dubhe departs from Puerto Banús, Marbella's famous marina. You'll meet the skipper at the port before heading out into the Mediterranean.

faq.q: Can I visit Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés on the Dubhe?
faq.a: Yes, the Dubhe is well-suited for visiting these nearby coves. Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés are just a short ride from Puerto Banús and offer crystal-clear water for swimming.

faq.q: Is there a minimum rental duration for the Dubhe?
faq.a: The standard minimum is 2 hours, though longer half-day and full-day charters are available. Contact us via WhatsApp for exact availability and pricing.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks on the Dubhe?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. The boat already includes complimentary drinks and snacks, but there is no restriction on extras.

faq.q: How do I book the Dubhe?
faq.a: Booking is easy: send us a WhatsApp message with your preferred date and duration. We'll confirm availability and provide a quote. Payment is secure and straightforward.

data.body_html:
If you are looking for a small, simple, and affordable boat in Marbella, the Dubhe is worth a close look. At 8 metres long and carrying a maximum of 5 passengers, it is the most compact and accessible vessel in our fleet at Boat Rental Marbella. This honest review covers what it is like onboard, where you can go, and whether it suits your trip.

What is the Dubhe?

The Dubhe is an 8-metre motorboat that departs from Puerto Banús. It is the cheapest hull in our fleet, making it a great entry-level option. It has an open layout with seating for five, a small sunpad area, and a basic bimini top for shade. There is no cabin or toilet, so it is strictly a day-boat for coastal cruising and swimming stops.

Who is the Dubhe for?

The Dubhe is ideal for couples, small families, or groups of friends who want a straightforward, no-fuss day on the water. It is not designed for parties or large groups — the 5-person limit keeps things intimate. At 8 metres it sits above Spain's 5 m licence-free limit, so you need a valid boating licence (PNB/PER, ICC or RYA Day Skipper) to take the helm — otherwise ask about a skippered departure. If you want to explore the coastline without the complexity of a big yacht, this is a solid choice.

What is it like onboard?

Onboard, the Dubhe feels compact but functional. The helm station is simple, with basic instrumentation and a single outboard engine. Seating is arranged around the stern and bow, and there is a small table for snacks and drinks. The sunpad at the bow is large enough for two people to lie down. Storage is limited, so pack light. The boat handles well in calm conditions and is easy to moor near coves.

Where can you go from Puerto Banús?

From Puerto Banús, the Dubhe can reach several popular spots within a short cruise. Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés are both less than 2 nautical miles away, offering clear water for swimming and snorkelling. Further south, you can head toward Estepona or north toward Marbella Marina. The boat’s shallow draft allows it to get close to beaches. For a longer trip, the Golden Mile coastline offers scenic views of La Concha mountain.

What is included in the charter?

Every Dubhe charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Fuel is not included (typically €30–€60 depending on route). Unlike our larger boats, the Dubhe does not include a jet ski or water toys. Pricing is published: €230 / 2h · €280 / 3h · €350 / 4h.

How does it compare to other boats in our fleet?

The Dubhe is the smallest and most affordable option in our fleet. By comparison, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) costs €749 for 2 hours and offers more space and comfort. The Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) is slightly shorter and sits in a similar price bracket. If you need more passenger capacity, consider the Fairline Targa 12m (10 pax) or the Azimut 39 (11 pax). For the biggest experience, the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) is a luxury option. Browse our full fleet at Boat Rental Marbella to compare.

Pros and cons of the Dubhe

  • Pros: Lowest price in the fleet, easy to handle, ideal for small groups, departs from Puerto Banús.
  • Cons: No cabin or toilet, limited storage, no water toys, 5-person max, fuel billed separately.

How to book the Dubhe

Booking the Dubhe is straightforward. Send us a WhatsApp message with your preferred date and duration. We will confirm availability and pricing (€230 / 2h · €280 / 3h · €350 / 4h). Payment is handled securely, and we will meet you at Puerto Banús on the day. For more details on our full fleet, visit our boat rental page or check our blog for other reviews and guides.

Frequently asked questions

Do I need a licence to drive the Dubhe in Marbella?

The Dubhe is 8 metres, above Spain's 5 m licence-free limit, so a valid boating licence (PNB/PER, ICC or RYA Day Skipper) is needed to take the helm. No licence? Ask us on WhatsApp about a skippered departure. A full safety briefing is given before departure either way.

How many people can the Dubhe carry?

The Dubhe has a maximum capacity of 5 passengers. This makes it ideal for couples, small families, or a group of friends looking for an intimate day out.

What is included in the Dubhe charter price?

Every Dubhe charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Fuel is not included (typically €30–€60 depending on route). Pricing is published: €230 for 2 hours, €280 for 3 hours, €350 for 4 hours.

Where does the Dubhe depart from?

The Dubhe departs from Puerto Banús, Marbella's famous marina. You'll meet the skipper at the port before heading out into the Mediterranean.

Can I visit Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés on the Dubhe?

Yes, the Dubhe is well-suited for visiting these nearby coves. Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés are just a short ride from Puerto Banús and offer crystal-clear water for swimming.

Is there a minimum rental duration for the Dubhe?

The standard minimum is 2 hours, though longer half-day and full-day charters are available. Contact us via WhatsApp for exact availability and pricing.

Can I bring my own food and drinks on the Dubhe?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. The boat already includes complimentary drinks and snacks, but there is no restriction on extras.

How do I book the Dubhe?

Booking is easy: send us a WhatsApp message with your preferred date and duration. We'll confirm availability and provide a quote. Payment is secure and straightforward.

For more honest reviews and guides, visit our blog or browse our full fleet.
